content.question: Do the short-term value-added gains for selected students in 6/8-year gimnázium tracks persist into upper-secondary completion, postsecondary attainment, and labor-market outcomes (wages, employment, occupational mobility), or do they dissipate as the initial cohort advantage narrows with age?
content.reduction_path: Extend Horn and Gurzó's follow-up thesis with longitudinal administrative matched data tracking selected and non-selected cohorts into age 25–35, measuring secondary completion, tertiary enrollment, earnings, and job stability to quantify whether short-term sorting persists into adulthood.
